new_classes.definition_en: Natural or artificial mineral water and aerated (carbonated) water, not containing added sugar, sweetening matter, or flavouring agents, classified under CN heading 2201 subheadings 2201 10 11 to 2201 10 90. Excludes natural mineral water presented in spray cans for skin care (heading 3304).
A quality borne by a water beverage indicating that it contains dissolved carbon dioxide, making it sparkling or effervescent, as opposed to still water.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the two main distinguishing criteria for CN 2201 10 11–90: (1) the product is mineral water or aerated/carbonated water, modelled as a subclass of WatersInclNaturalArtificialMineralWaters2201 bearing a CarbonatedQuality, and (2) it does not contain added sugar or flavouring (complement restriction on SweetenedOrFlavouredQuality). The exclusion of spray-can skin-care products (heading 3304) cannot be fully axiomatised in OWL without a purpose/use modelling pattern not currently present in the TBox, so this exclusion criterion reduces the score. Additionally, the specific subdivision between 2201 10 11 (natural mineral water) and 2201 10 90 (other, e.g. artificial mineral water) is not discriminated here as it would require a further 'natural origin' quality class not yet in the TBox; this is a gap at the 8-digit level rather than the subheading level covered by this note.
